items.title: UKM first in Asean to integrate BlackBerry's QNX programme into curriculum
items.summary: BANGI: Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia (UKM) has become the first university in Asean to integrate BlackBerry's QNX Everywhere programme and QNX foundational operating system to strengthen Malaysia's software engineering talent pipeline. BlackBerry vice-president of QNX engineering and head of research and development for Asia-Pacific Raj Jain said the programme would equip future engineers with practical experience in developing software for safety-critical industries. "With QNX Everywhere, we are giving Malaysia's next generation of engineers hands-on experience with the trusted software foundation that powers safe, secure, real-time systems," he said.

items.title: Trust crisis deepens as AI reshapes PR landscape
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The public relations (PR) industry must navigate a new era of artificial intelligence (AI), misinformation and digital disruption as technology reshapes how organisations communicate and build trust. The conference, organised by World Communications, brought together representatives from multinational corporations, government ministries, government-linked companies, media organisations, universities and regional PR associations. The event focused on public trust, misinformation, digital manipulation and the rapid adoption of AI tools.

items.title: Selangor Sultan disappointed by criticism of state flagpole
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The flying of the Jalur Gemilang and the Selangor state flag on towering flagpoles is not merely about creating a physical structure or landmark, but serves as a symbol of patriotism, loyalty and unity that should be cherished, the Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, said. In a post on the Selangor Royal Office's Facebook page, the ruler said such displays could strengthen patriotic values and instil pride in both the state and the nation. Sultan Sharafuddin said he hoped the example set by Istanbul would inspire efforts to cultivate patriotism and strengthen unity among Malaysians.

items.title: Perak flags concerns over Rohingya presence in Manjung
items.summary: Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad said the situation was under close monitoring by the authorities amid concerns over the growing numbers and the authenticity of documents carried by members of the community. Saarani said the matter had also been raised in National Security Council (NSC) discussions, alongside concerns voiced by local residents. Saarani said there was information indicating the existence of makeshift homes and informal schooling within the community.

items.title: RM9m in alleged false claims: MACC remands 73 agents, company owners in Daya Kerjaya probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 11 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has remanded 73 individuals, comprising agents and company owners, on suspicion of falsifying Daya Kerjaya 2.0 incentive claims worth about RM9 million. The source said MACC also recorded 104 statements during the operation, involving 32 persons under investigation and 72 witnesses. “Through this operation, MACC also froze 25 company bank accounts containing about RM390,000,” the source said.

items.title: Peugeot engulfed in flames on Federal Highway
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A Peugeot was almost completely destroyed in a fire along the Federal Highway near the Mercedes-Benz showroom, heading from Kuala Lumpur towards Shah Alam, today. The Selangor Fire and Rescue Department said it received a distress call at 1.15pm and dispatched a team from the Petaling Jaya Fire and Rescue Station to the scene. "The fire engine arrived at 1.28pm and found the Peugeot engulfed in flames.

items.title: Penang High Court upholds dog‑beating convictions, reduces fines from RM50,000 to RM30,000 each
items.summary: Justice Choong Yeow Choy dismissed Rosli Mat Zin and Suhaili Haron’s appeal against their conviction under Section 29 (1) of the Animal Welfare Act 2015, finding that the conviction was grounded on admissible direct evidence and properly admitted video evidence. However, he allowed their appeal against the sentence and varied the RM50,000 fine in default 12 months’ imprisonment imposed by the Sessions Court with a fine of RM30,000 each, in default six months’ imprisonment. He said that no provision in the Animal Welfare Act restricted the police from handling cases arising under the Act.

items.title: ToYm ecosystem brands earn Asean Records recognition via online-to-offline retail campaign
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Three brands under the Thousands Of Years Media (ToYm) ecosystem have achieved Asean Records recognition through an integrated online-to-offline retail conversion campaign. The achievement by ToYm Micro Drama Ads, ToYm Wardrobe and Dong Lin Fish Scale Jelly was recorded during the opening of the first ToYm Wardrobe physical store in Bukit Jalil recently. ToYm, ToYm Wardrobe and ToYm Beaute have collectively received seven Malaysia Book of Records titles across micro drama productions, digital content initiatives, retail activations and consumer engagement campaigns.

items.title: High Court upholds conviction of 2 men for beating dog, reduces fine to RM30,000 each
items.summary: Justice Choong Yeow Choy dismissed Rosli Mat Zin and Suhaili Haron's appeal against their conviction under Section 29 (1) of the Animal Welfare Act 2015, finding that the conviction was grounded on admissible direct evidence and properly admitted video evidence. However, he allowed their appeal against the sentence and varied the RM50,000 fine in default 12 months' imprisonment imposed by the Sessions Court with a fine of RM30,000 each, in default six months' imprisonment. He said that no provision in the Animal Welfare Act restricted the police from handling cases arising under the Act.

items.title: English teacher claims trial to sexually assaulting 9-year-old pupil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: An English teacher pleaded not guilty at the Ampang Sessions Court today to charges of sexually assaulting his 9-year-old pupil twice last month. The charges were framed under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act which provides a maximum 20-year jail term and whipping upon conviction. Deputy public prosecutor Muhammad Hairuliqram Hairuddin requested bail of RM20,000 with one surety for both charges.

items.title: Deputy IGP says fentanyl‑based ‘Piu Piu’ leaves users zombie‑like, calls for total vape ban
items.summary: Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay said the drug, a mixture of fentanyl and psychoactive chemicals, could cause severe intoxication effects, leaving users in a zombie-like state. He said the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) viewed vape usage as having deviated from its original purpose and being widely abused by drug trafficking syndicates as a modus operandi to evade detection by the authorities. Earlier Ayob Khan officiated a hospital visit programme held in conjunction with the 219th Police Day celebration at Hospital Tunku Ampuan Besar Tuanku Aishah Rohani, the Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia Specialist Children’s Hospital (HPKK UKM), here.

items.title: Evicted tenant jailed after failing to pay RM4,300 over slashed car tyres
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: A restaurant worker who slashed all four tyres of a woman's car after being evicted from a rented room was fined RM4,000 by the magistrate's court here today. Magistrate Arun Noval Dass also ordered M. Yuvarajana, 36, to serve five months in jail if he failed to pay the fine after he pleaded guilty to a charge of committing mischief. The court further ordered the father of one to pay RM300 in compensation to the victim, or serve one month's imprisonment in default.

items.title: Ampang English teacher pleads not guilty to charges of sexually assaulting nine‑year‑old pupil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 11 — A male teacher pleaded not guilty at the Ampang Sessions Court to two charges of committing physical sexual assault against a female pupil last month. Mohd Saufi Jama’in, 45, is accused of committing the acts against a nine-year-old girl, with whom the accused had a relationship of trust as her English teacher. Earlier, deputy public prosecutor Muhammad Hairuliqram Hairuddin offered bail of RM20,000 with one surety for both charges, along with additional conditions that the accused must report to the nearest police station once a month and refrain from contacting or disturbing the victim and tampering with witnesses.

items.title: AirAsia X postpones Bahrain flight route due to regional conflict
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian low-cost airline AirAsia X said on Thursday it has postponed the launch of its planned service via Bahrain until at least August due to the conflict in the Middle East. AirAsia initially planned to commence the route in June, linking Kuala Lumpur to Bahrain and onward to London's Gatwick airport. The airline said affected passengers would be offered refunds or rescheduled bookings.

items.title: Discipline teacher allegedly attacked after cutting student's hair
items.summary: Perak Education, Higher Education, Youth and Sports Committee chairman Datuk Khairuddin Abu Hanipah said he was awaiting a detailed report from the Perak Education Department before issuing any statement. He told reporters this after officiating the Tabika Kemas Gen Alpha Haj Simulation Educational Excellence Programme at Masjid Sultan Yusuff Izzudin Shah in Seri Iskandar today. At the event, he said about 400 children from 30 Tabika Kemas centres in Perak Tengah took part in a one-day haj simulation programme organised in collaboration with Andalusia Travel & Tours Sdn Bhd.

items.title: Loke vows firm action against RTD enforcement officers involved in bribery
items.summary: NILAI: The Road Transport Department (RTD) will take firm action against any enforcement officer guilty of bribery, says Transport Minister Anthony Loke. He said the department maintains a strict zero-tolerance stance on corruption and will not compromise on any proven misconduct involving its personnel. Loke also urged members of the public with evidence of wrongdoing to channel it directly to the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) for further action.

items.title: New RM140mil RTD complex in Negri Sembilan next year – Loke
items.summary: NILAI: A new RM140 million Road Transport Department (RTD) complex will be built in Negri Sembilan, with construction set to begin next year. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said it will be developed on the existing site in Senawang under Rolling Plan 1 of the 13th Malaysia Plan. "The existing RTD building in Senawang will be demolished to make way for the new complex on the same site," he told reporters after officiating the RTD Nilai branch office here today.

items.title: Perak police continue probe despite apology over TikTok claims defaming Tok Batin, hiker Jaslinda
items.summary: IPOH, June 11 — Police will continue investigating a man who allegedly posted defamatory content about the Tok Batin of Kampung Orang Asli Lubuk Gaharu, Pos Musoh, linked to the discovery of hiker Jaslinda Saludin, despite his public apology. Tapah district police chief Supt Abdul Malik Hasim said investigations will be conducted under Section 500 of the Penal Code and Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998, following 11 police reports lodged by various parties, including a group of Tok Batin, over the issue. Yesterday, the man issued a public apology via a video recording following controversy over a livestream on his TikTok account, which allegedly defamed Mohd Asmmadi and Jaslinda.

items.title: Malaysia's SD Guthrie working with Indonesia on palm oil plantation seizures, CEO says
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's SD Guthrie is working with the Indonesian government on the issue of land seizuresaffecting some of its palm oil plantations, its chief executive Mohd Haris Mohd Arshad said on Thursday. Indonesia launched a task force last year consisting of the military, the police and state prosecutors to crack down on violations in the palm oil industry. The task force seized around 4.1 million hectares of plantations last year for operating illegally in forest areas, a move that affected both major palm oil companies and smallholder farmers.

items.title: Unemployment rate back at 3pct in April
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's unemployment rate returned to 3.0 per cent in April, with 511,800 unemployed persons recorded, according to the Statistics Department. "In the meantime, the number of employed persons continued to increase in April 2026, rising by 0.1 per cent to 16.82 million persons against the previous month (March 2026: 16.80 million persons). "Likewise, the number of unemployed persons remained on an upward trajectory, edging up by 0.6 per cent to 511.8 thousand persons, compared to 509.0 thousand persons in the prior month," it said.

items.title: Rohingya man charged with murder after fellow countryman stabbed to death in Seberang Perai
items.summary: NIBONG TEBAL, June 11 — A Rohingya man was charged at the Jawi Magistrate’s Court here today with the murder of his fellow countryman in a residential area last month. Osman Bawdi Alom, 38, nodded to indicate he understood after the charge was read in Malay before Magistrate Nurul Aina Ahmad. He is accused of murdering Abdul Rahman Shah Mia, 31, at Lorong Belibis 14, Taman Belibis, South Seberang Perai, here on May 31.

items.title: Man, 67, remanded in Batu Pahat over alleged rape of 13-year-old granddaughter
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 11 — A 67-year-old man has reportedly been remanded for six days in Batu Pahat on suspicion of raping his 13-year-old granddaughter last year. According to Utusan Malaysia , Batu Pahat district police chief Assistant Commissioner Shahrulanuar Mushaddat Abdullah Sani confirmed that police had arrested the elderly suspect, who is now under remand. Police reportedly received a report two days ago after the victim told her mother about the alleged abuse.

items.title: Siblings arrested over viral nasi bajet stall theft in Ipoh
items.summary: IPOH: Police have arrested two siblings suspected of stealing a cash box and a mobile phone from a nasi bajet trader at Jalan Lebuh Meru Raya here last month. "While she was busy packing nasi lemak, one of the suspects took a cash box containing cash and a mobile phone before both fled the scene," he said in a statement today. Najib said both suspects had admitted their involvement in the theft.

items.title: [UPDATED] MACC detains 73 nationwide over alleged RM9mil Daya Kerjaya claims fraud
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has remanded 73 people, including agents and company owners, over alleged fraudulent claims totalling about RM9 million under the Daya Kerjaya 2.0 employment incentive programme. Sources said the suspects, comprising 48 men and 25 women aged between their 20s and 70s, were detained on Tuesday at MACC offices nationwide, except in Perlis. Sources said intelligence gathered since November last year had identified 143 company premises and 320 people allegedly linked to the fraudulent claims.

items.title: Three friends, including teenager, charged with gang-raping 13-year-old girl
items.summary: The 18-year-old teenager, together with Mohamad Aiman Firdaus Nazri, 24, and Muhammad Hakimi Razlan, 23, pleaded not guilty after the charges were read separately before judge Ahmad Bazli Bahruddin. Mohamad Aiman and Muhammad Hakimi allegedly committed the same offence at an abandoned house in Kampung Sungai Tias, Laloh, between 1am and 1.30am on May 28. The other two accused were represented by lawyer Muhammad Izzat Hasizan.

items.title: Man pleads not guilty to drink-driving charge, granted RM12,000 bail
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: An eatery assistant pleaded not guilty at the magistrate's court here today to a charge of driving under the influence of alcohol last month. The bachelor was accused of driving while intoxicated, with a blood alcohol level of 192mg/100ml, which exceeded the permitted limit of 50mg/100ml. After identifying themselves, police conducted a breathalyser test and found the accused was driving under the influence of alcohol.

items.title: Driver found dead after Toyota Innova bursts into flames in early‑morning crash with trailer near Pantai Remis
items.summary: IPOH, June 11 — A driver was found dead after the vehicle he was travelling in caught fire following a crash involving a sport utility vehicle (SUV) and a trailer lorry along Jalan Batu 9, Kampung Semangat, Segari, near Pantai Remis early this morning. Perak Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said the department received an emergency call regarding the incident at 4.36am, prompting a team from the Pantai Remis Fire and Rescue Station to be dispatched to the scene. “After the fire was extinguished, rescue personnel discovered a victim trapped inside the vehicle.

items.title: Caught with 490 turtle eggs, man gets 18 months’ jail and RM50,000 fine in Sabah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 11 — A foreign man has reportedly been sentenced to 18 months’ jail and fined RM50,000 after pleading guilty to possessing 490 turtle eggs from fully protected species in Sabah. According to Sinar Harian , the accused, Manan Saibung, who had no identification documents, admitted to the charge before Sessions Court Judge Roslinda Razali in Sandakan on Thursday. He was reportedly found with eggs from the green turtle (Chelonia mydas) and hawksbill turtle (Eretmochelys imbricata) species without valid permission.

items.title: Car salesman fined, jailed, for having nude videos of 9-year-old boy
items.summary: MUAR: 26-year-old car salesman was sentenced to six months' jail and fined RM3,000 by the magistrate's court here today for possessing child sexual abuse material (CSAM). Magistrate Kartini Kasran meted out the sentence against Tan Yean Xuen, who pleaded guilty to possessing nude photos and videos of a 9-year-old boy. An inspection of Tan's mobile phone later revealed several photos and videos of the child.

items.title: Man fined RM100 for swearing at cop
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 25-year-old restaurant assistant was fined the maximum of RM100 by the magistrate's court today after admitting to hurling vulgarities at a police corporal during a traffic enforcement operation in Sentul on Monday. Magistrate Nurul Izzah Hasan Basri ordered Muhammad Syarifuddin Abd Latif to serve a day in jail if he fails to pay the fine. Syarifuddin pleaded guilty to using insulting words intended to provoke a breach of peace against a 27-year-old policeman attached to the motorcycle patrol unit of Sentul district police headquarters.

items.title: Kelantan govt provides rental aid to Kampung Cina fire victims
items.summary: GUA MUSANG: The Kelantan government has agreed to cover house rental costs for three months for 27 families affected by the recent fire at Kampung Cina in Kota Baru. Menteri Besar Datuk Mohd Nassuruddin Daud said the aid was intended to ease the burden on victims who lost their homes and belongings in the blaze. Nassuruddin said the state government understood the difficulties faced by the victims and remained committed to safeguarding their welfare.

items.title: PAC finalises report on airport management after KLIA visit
items.summary: SEPANG: The Public Accounts Committee (PAC) is in the final stages of completing its report on the management of public airports. PAC chairman Datuk Mas Ermieyati Samsudin said a working visit to Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) today would help the committee finalise a report that is "more transparent, comprehensive and thorough" following its inquiry into airport operations and infrastructure projects. She said the visit to KLIA formed part of the committee's ongoing inquiry into airport management involving the Finance Ministry, Transport Ministry and Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB).

items.title: Unemployed man charged with murder after acquaintance found dead in rolled‑up mattress in Melaka
items.summary: MELAKA, June 11 — An unemployed man was charged at the Ayer Keroh Magistrate’s Court here today with the murder of an acquaintance at a shophouse in March. Kamil Harris Mohd Abd Nasir, 40, nodded in understanding after the charge was read before Magistrate Uthman Abd Ghani. He is accused of murdering Mohd Idris Yasmir, 40, at Taman Seri Pandan 1, Jalan Pokok Mangga here between 10pm, March 30 and 3am, March 31.

items.title: Pasir Gudang chemical leak sends yellow smoke into air
items.summary: PASIR GUDANG: Yellow smoke filled the air after a tank containing spent inorganic acid leaked at an industrial facility here today. No casualties were reported after the 200-litre tank containing the chemical at an industrial waste management facility leaked this morning. Spent inorganic acid is a hazardous industrial waste derived from strong acids such as sulphuric or hydrochloric mixtures.

items.title: Johor grandfather child abuse case: Nancy says ministry ready to assist victim's family
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Women, Family and Community Development Ministry will leave investigations into the alleged sexual abuse of a 13-year-old girl by her grandfather in Johor to the relevant authorities. However, Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri said the ministry stands ready to assist the victim's family, particularly in providing counselling services. "However, we will also take the necessary steps to obtain reports on the case," she said.

items.title: Multi-agency approach needed to address Rohingya issue, says Ayob Khan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Rohingya refugee issue requires a comprehensive, multi-agency approach involving countries across the region and cannot be resolved through police enforcement alone. Citing United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) data as of April, Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay said Malaysia has 219,472 registered UNHCR cardholders, of whom 197,591 are Myanmar nationals, including 128,200 Rohingya. "Of the total, 197,591 were Myanmar nationals, with 128,200 from the Rohingya community," he said.

items.title: Students unhappy over haircut, discipline teacher allegedly ends up punched in Kuala Kangsar
items.summary: The incident, which occurred around 11.30am, is said to have been triggered when the teacher cut the hair of one of the students involved, Sinar Harian (SH) reported. Perak executive councillor for education, higher learning, youth and sports Datuk Khairuddin Abu Hanipah said the state government is awaiting an official report from the Perak Education Department (JPN). Khairuddin added that students must respect their teachers even if certain aspects of the learning process may be disagreeable.

items.title: Tun Sharifah Rodziah sea base decommissioned after a decade in Semporna waters
items.summary: SEMPORNA: The Tun Sharifah Rodziah Sea Base (PL TSR), located in the waters off Semporna, was officially decommissioned yesterday after serving for a decade since July 31, 2015 as a forward operating base safeguarding security and sovereignty in the country's waters off eastern Sabah. The statement said that throughout its decade of service, PL TSR had served as a strategic platform supporting maritime security operations, monitoring areas of maritime interest and strengthening the presence of security forces in the Eastern Sabah Security Zone (ESS Zone). It said the base's strategic location enabled rapid responses to security threats and symbolised the RMN's continued commitment to defending national sovereignty.

items.title: UM can break into world's top 50 universities, says Zambry
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Universiti Malaya can break into the world's top 50 universities from its current 58th position, says Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Zambry Abd Kadir. He said while UM's current position was an achievement, the institution was capable of meeting higher targets. Zambry said that every instance of progress and change in the history of man began with an idea, and that creative and innovative ideas can drive a rise in rankings.

items.title: Vietnam's Vingroup forges ahead with 'world's largest stadium' despite demand doubts
items.summary: HANOI: Vietnam's Vingroup is accelerating construction in Hanoi of what it says will be the world's largest stadium, betting that future demand will emerge to make the 135,000-seat venue financially viable over the long term. The stadium will have "the world's largest seating capacity" and will also feature the biggest fully retractable roof, said Vingroup, Vietnam's largest firm by market capitalisation, which is simultaneously working on multiple projects, including a high-speed railway, urban developments and wind power plants. Vingroup said the project's scale reflects plans to host large sporting and cultural events, including concerts.

items.title: Ministry urges states to raise Muslim marriage age to 18
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Women, Family and Community Development Ministry has urged state governments to raise the minimum age of Muslim marriage to 18. Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri said she hoped other states would follow Selangor and Kedah in raising the minimum marriage age to enable nationwide standardisation. "We hope that all states will take similar action so that the matter can be standardised and harmonised nationwide, as Muslim marriage falls under the jurisdiction of the state government," she said.

items.title: No more cash counters: EPF moves contributions to apps, banks and self‑service terminals from July 1
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, June 11 — The Employees Provident Fund (EPF) will discontinue remittance counters at all branches nationwide from July 1, 2026, shifting contribution payments fully to digital and alternative channels. The retirement fund said other service counters would continue operating as usual, The Star reported. The EPF stressed that the closure of receipting counters would not affect members’ or employers’ ability to make payments, as multiple alternative channels remain available.

items.title: Knife‑wielding couple nabbed after Grab driver’s throat slashed at Kota Tinggi bus terminal
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, June 11 — Police arrested a couple after they were believed to be involved in a knife-wielding robbery of a ride-sharing driver at the Tun Sri Lanang bus terminal here yesterday. Kota Tinggi police chief Superintendent Yusof Othman said the couple, both aged 20, were nabbed in separate locations by policemen from the Criminal Investigation Division (CID) yesterday. “Police also seized a knife, suspected to be the weapon used in the robbery attempt, from the couple,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Bersatu vows to defend Endau seat as Alwiyah Talib exits, Azmin rallies grassroots support
items.summary: Bersatu secretary-general Datuk Seri Mohamed Azmin Ali said Endau remains the party’s stronghold and grassroots support is still intact. “Mersing still remains a Bersatu stronghold. “The Mersing parliamentary seat and Endau state seat were won by Bersatu.

items.title: Police report filed after Albert Tei stages four protests at Menara Komunikasi, J‑KOM cites obstruction
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 11 — The Community Communications Department (J-KOM) today lodged a police report against activist Albert Tei over allegations that he had disrupted operations at the Ministry of Communications through a series of gatherings held since last month. J-KOM director-general Mohd Hisyamuddin Ghazali said the report was lodged after the individual allegedly staged several gatherings in front of the ministry building, affecting the smooth conduct of duties by civil servants. He said the report to MCMC would involve Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998, which concerns the improper use of network facilities or network services to harass or provoke others.

items.title: ‘Perikatan does not belong to any single party’: Ahmad Samsuri says PAS a founding member too
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 11 — Perikatan Nasional (PN) chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar today stressed that PAS is also a founding member of the opposition coalition, saying PN does not belong exclusively to any single party amid lingering questions over the bloc’s future. PN is a constitutional political coalition established on the basis of consensus among its founding parties. Samsuri’s remarks come two days after Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin said his party would remain in PN despite PAS ending political cooperation with his party, insisting that Bersatu was a founding member of the coalition.

items.title: Malaysia’s jobless rate edges back to 3.0pc in April, 511,800 unemployed as labour force grows
items.summary: Chief Statistician Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin said the number of employed persons continued to increase in April 2026, rising by 0.1 per cent to 16.82 million persons against the previous month (March 2026: 16.80 million persons). “The employees’ category constituted 75.0 per cent of total employed persons in April 2026. This category increased marginally by 0.05 per cent to 12.61 million persons (March 2026: 12.60 million persons).

items.title: Politicians come and go, institutions remain: Leaders call for stronger federal-state framework
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 11 — Malaysia needs a stronger rules-based system to ensure the interests of every state are protected regardless of which political party is in power, state leaders at the Inter-State Forum on State-Federal Relations 2026 said today. Leaders from several states, including Penang, Sarawak, Selangor, Kelantan, Terengganu and Perlis, said stronger institutions, constitutional safeguards and clearer mechanisms governing federal-state relations were needed to reduce the adverse effects of political changes on governance and development. But institutions remain,” he said.

items.title: Tunku Zain named new Tunku Panglima Besar of Negeri Sembilan
items.summary: KUALA PILAH, June 10 — Yang Dipertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir today proclaimed his second son Tunku Zain Al-’Abidin as the holder of the hereditary title of Putera Yang Empat, Tunku Panglima Besar of Negeri Sembilan, with immediate effect. The proclamation ceremony was held in the Balairong Seri, Istana Besar Seri Menanti, with Tunku Ampuan Besar Negeri Sembilan Tuanku Aishah Rohani Tengku Besar Mahmud and Tunku Besar Seri Menanti Tunku Ali Redhauddin Tuanku Muhriz in attendance. The proclamation was made to fill the vacancy in the hereditary title of Tunku Panglima Besar of Negeri Sembilan, which was previously held by Tunku Datuk Seri Nadzaruddin Tuanku Ja’afar.

items.title: King revokes Isa Samad’s ‘Tan Sri’ title over corruption conviction
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 11 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has revoked the Panglima Setia Mahkota award conferred on former Felda chairman Mohd Isa Abdul Samad. In a statement today, the Prime Minister’s Department said the award, which carries the title “Tan Sri”, was revoked effective June 8. Mohd Isa, a former Negri Sembilan menteri besar, is serving a six-year jail sentence after the Federal Court in February reinstated his corruption conviction and RM15.45 million fine over nine corruption charges linked to Felda Investment Corporation’s purchase of a hotel.

items.title: KTMB says JB Sentral stays open, only Kempas Baru–JB track shut overnight, services resume 6am
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 11 — Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad (KTMB) has clarified that the temporary closure of the track between the Kempas Baru Station and JB Sentral Station does not involve a full shutdown of JB Sentral’s operations. In a statement today, KTMB said the closure will take place from 10pm tonight until 6am tomorrow to allow for the safe launching of a Pedestrian Over Bridge at JB Sentral Station. “Both trains will terminate at Kempas Baru Station, and passengers will be transported to JB Sentral Station using replacement bus services provided by KTMB.

items.title: Police raid in Kota Kinabalu seizes 159.8kg meth worth RM7.99m, drugs hidden in Perodua Alza
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 11 — Police seized 159.8kg of suspected methamphetamine worth an estimated RM7.99 million and arrested two men and a woman in a series of raids under Op Bayu 9 around the city on May 22. Sabah Police Commissioner Datuk Jauteh Dikun said the suspects, aged between 18 and 49, were detained following intelligence-led operations by the Sabah Narcotics Crime Investigation Department. He said the first suspect was arrested at 3.40pm near City Mall while carrying a box containing nine packages of suspected syabu weighing about 9.1kg.

items.title: Pakatan not eyeing post-poll pact with BN in Johor and Negeri Sembilan, says Amanah sec-gen
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 11 — Amanah secretary-general Muhammad Faiz Fadzil has reportedly said Pakatan Harapan (PH) is not planning any post-election cooperation with Barisan Nasional (BN) after the Johor and Negeri Sembilan state polls. According to Utusan Malaysia , Faiz said PH’s current priority was to strengthen its own position ahead of any political developments after the elections. He said Amanah was confident it would be allocated seats it could win once negotiations within PH were finalised.

items.title: CAAM reminds AirBorneo of consumer obligations as Sabah, Sarawak flights disrupted
items.summary: KUCHING, June 11 — The Civil Aviation Authority of Malaysia (CAAM) is closely monitoring recent flight disruptions involving AirBorneo services across Sabah and Sarawak and has reminded the airline of its obligations to consumers under the Malaysian Aviation Consumer Protection Code 2016 (MACPC). In a statement, CAAM said the flight delays, cancellations and schedule changes were attributed by AirBorneo to operational and maintenance-related issues. While stressing that safety must remain the highest priority in flight operations, the aviation regulator said affected passengers must continue to be treated fairly and in accordance with consumer protection requirements.

items.title: Heavy rain triggers flash floods in Bintulu, vehicles stranded as water spills into homes
items.summary: BINTULU, June 11 — Several roads and residential areas here have been inundated following heavy rain since early Thursday morning. The downpour caused flash floods in a number of low-lying areas, with floodwaters rising rapidly and affecting traffic flow on several roads across the town. Among the affected areas were residential neighbourhoods where overflowing drains led to water spilling onto roads and into house compounds.

items.title: Sarawak JKR hands over RM100m schools in Bintulu to MOE, boosting education demand
items.summary: BINTULU, June 11 — The Sarawak Public Works Department (JKR) has handed over two new school projects here worth more than RM100 million to the Ministry of Education (MOE) to meet the growing demand for educational facilities in rapidly developing areas in Bintulu. In a statement, Sarawak JKR said the projects handed over were Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Samalaju, valued at RM57.62 million and Sekolah Kebangsaan (SK) Orang Kaya Mohammad Bintulu, worth RM42.74 million. Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Samalaju.

items.title: Anwar hails Malaysia–Japan ties as vital amid global uncertainty, energy security and regional stability
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 11 — The close relationship between Malaysia and Japan is becoming increasingly important amid a more challenging and uncertain global environment, with both countries sharing common interests in strengthening energy security, economic resilience, technological development and regional stability, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. In a Facebook post yesterday, he said his first official visit to Japan, which began on Monday and lasted three days, marked an important milestone in further strengthening the special relationship between the two countries, since diplomatic ties were established in 1957. Anwar said Malaysia and Japan had since continued to grow as close friends and trusted strategic partners.

items.title: MCMC says reliable telecoms vital for RTS Link success, highlights collaboration during Johor site visit
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 11 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) has committed to fully supporting the upcoming Johor Bahru-Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link by providing reliable digital connectivity infrastructure to ensure smooth operations of the cross-border rail network, according to a post on the RTS Link JB-SG official Facebook page. He highlighted this during a working visit by an MCMC delegation to the RTS Link project site at the Bukit Chagar Station and Bangunan Sultan Ibrahim yesterday to gain firsthand insight into the project’s progress and ongoing developments. The MCMC delegation was welcomed by the RTS Link’s Electrical, Mechanical and Building Services (EMBS) Project Director Ir Ardy Suhaidy, and Health, Environment, Safety and Security Chief Syahmarul Zulaihan Marlap.

items.title: Selangor MB says enforcement on illegal Rohingya flats in Hulu Langat dates back to 2016
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 11 — Various actions have been taken by government agencies since 2016 to address issues involving a residential building occupied by Rohingya residents in Kampung Baru Sungai Makau, Mukim Ulu Langat, which recently went viral on social media. Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said the measures included the issuance of warning notices, compounds, integrated operations, investigations and enforcement action by various government agencies. “Various measures have been taken since 2016, including the issuance of warning notices, compounds, integrated operations, investigations and enforcement action by the Hulu Langat District and Land Office (PDT), Kajang Municipal Council (MPKj), Selangor Water Management Authority (LUAS), Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM), the Immigration Department, as well as demolition operations,” he said in a Facebook post.

items.title: PM Anwar stresses Unity Government stability, rejects talk of betrayal as PAS ends Bersatu ties
items.summary: TOKYO, June 11 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today reaffirmed the Unity Government’s commitment to its coalition partners, saying it would not take any action that could be perceived as a betrayal of the existing political consensus. Anwar, who is Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman, said the government remained stable, with all parties continuing to respect the understanding reached to ensure the country’s political stability. Anwar, who is also PKR president, was commenting on claims that several former Bersatu state assemblymen could join PKR following recent developments in the political relationship between Bersatu and PAS.
